user_message: Quick one -- build the analysis on Alpine Ski House. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 13:50 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 12:12 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-02-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Kevin (Alpine Ski House)

[00:02:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:07:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:12:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:17:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:19:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:21:00] Kevin (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:24:00] Kevin (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:26:00] Kevin (Alpine Ski House): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-C trap (Generates generic talking points); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-C failure mode (Generates generic talking points)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-C -- Generates generic talking points
